title: boswellia scopulorum
intro: Boswellia scopulorum, also known as the Mexican frankincense or Mexican boswellia, is a type of tree that belongs to the Burseraceae family. It is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, and its resin has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties. The resin of Boswellia scopulorum is valued for its anti-inflammatory, anti-oxidant, and anti-bacterial properties, which can help alleviate pain, reduce inflammation, and promote overall health. It can be prepared into herbal remedies such as essential oils, capsules, and teas, which have been used to treat various health conditions, including arthritis, digestive issues, and stress-related disorders.
